political_stances.stance_content: Implemented the Complementarity Assessment Framework (COMPASS) for Employment Pass holders to ensure foreign workforce complements local workforce.
Strengthened workplace safety and health regulations following high-profile workplace fatalities.
Progressively raising the retirement and re-employment ages to support an ageing workforce.
Strong advocate of tripartite collaboration between government, employers, and unions as the cornerstone of Singapore's labour relations.

major_achievements.achievement: Served as Group CEO of one of Asia's largest integrated private healthcare groups before entering politics
Entered politics and was quickly appointed to ministerial positions
Appointed Minister for Manpower, overseeing labour policy for Singapore
Implemented the COMPASS framework for Employment Pass holders

biography_summary: Tan See Leng is a Singaporean politician and former medical practitioner who has served as Minister for Manpower since 2021 and Minister-in-charge of Energy and Science and Technology since 2025. A doctor by training (MBBS, MMed from NUS) and member of the Royal College of Physicians (UK), he had a distinguished healthcare career, serving as Group CEO and Managing Director of Parkway Pantai Limited, one of Asia's largest integrated private healthcare groups. He entered politics in 2020 as a PAP candidate for Marine Parade GRC and was quickly promoted to full Minister for Manpower in 2021. He has implemented the COMPASS framework for Employment Pass holders and advocates tripartite collaboration.

collection_meta.notes: Enriched with career background (Parkway Pantai CEO), political stances on foreign workforce/COMPASS/workplace safety, major achievements from Wikipedia and web search
